req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-010 specifies a card freeze/unfreeze state machine with instant updates and network notification. Test suite covers: (1) happy path freeze→unfreeze transitions validating state changes, (2) negative paths for non-existent cards and authorization rejection, (3) boundary conditions for rapid state toggles and edge case card IDs. Heuristics applied: EP partitions valid/invalid card states; STC validates state transitions; BVA tests UUID boundary formats; NEG validates error codes; DT covers freeze→auth rejection logic.

tests.name: test_freeze_card_succeeds_and_updates_state
preconditions: Card with ID <valid_card_id> exists and belongs to authenticated customer
tests.executable:
def test_freeze_card_succeeds_and_updates_state():
    import requests
    card_id = '<valid_card_id>'
    auth_header = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{card_id}/freeze',
        json={'action': 'freeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['status'] == 'FROZEN'
    assert data['card_id'] == card_id
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{card_id}',
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()['state'] == 'FROZEN'

tests.name: test_frozen_card_rejects_authorization_attempt
preconditions: Card exists and belongs to authenticated customer
trace: R-FIN-010: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
tests.executable:
def test_frozen_card_rejects_authorization_attempt():
    import requests
    card_id = '<valid_card_id>'
    auth_header = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <token>'}
    freeze_resp = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{card_id}/freeze',
        json={'action': 'freeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    auth_resp = requests.post(
        'http://localhost:8000/transactions/authorize',
        json={'card_id': card_id, 'amount': 100},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code == 403
    assert 'frozen' in auth_resp.json()['error'].lower()

tests.name: test_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Card ID does not exist in system
expected: HTTP response status is 404 Not Found
trace: R-FIN-010: Error pathways: 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
def test_nonexistent_card_returns_404():
    import requests
    nonexistent_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
    auth_header = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{nonexistent_id}/freeze',
        json={'action': 'freeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 404
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['error_code'] == 'CARD_NOT_FOUND'
    assert 'card' in data['message'].lower()

tests.name: test_freeze_unfreeze_toggle_idempotency
preconditions: Card exists and belongs to authenticated customer
trace: Verify state machine boundaries and idempotency
tests.executable:
def test_freeze_unfreeze_toggle_idempotency():
    import requests
    card_id = '<valid_card_id>'
    auth_header = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <token>'}
    r1 = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{card_id}/freeze',
        json={'action': 'freeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert r1.status_code == 200
    assert r1.json()['status'] == 'FROZEN'
    r2 = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{card_id}/freeze',
        json={'action': 'freeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()['status'] == 'FROZEN'
    r3 = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze',
        json={'action': 'unfreeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    assert r3.json()['status'] == 'ACTIVE'
    r4 = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze',
        json={'action': 'unfreeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert r4.status_code == 200
    assert r4.json()['status'] == 'ACTIVE'

tests.name: test_unauthorized_customer_cannot_freeze_others_card
preconditions: Card exists but belongs to different customer
Requester is authenticated as different user
tests.executable:
def test_unauthorized_customer_cannot_freeze_others_card():
    import requests
    other_customer_card = '<other_customer_card_id>'
    auth_header = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <different_user_token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{other_customer_card}/freeze',
        json={'action': 'freeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    assert resp.json()['error_code'] == 'UNAUTHORIZED_CARD_ACCESS'

tests.name: test_invalid_card_id_format_rejected
expected: HTTP response status is 400 Bad Request
Response contains error indicating invalid card ID format
tests.executable:
def test_invalid_card_id_format_rejected():
    import requests
    invalid_id = 'not-a-uuid'
    auth_header = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'http://localhost:8000/cards/{invalid_id}/freeze',
        json={'action': 'freeze'},
        headers=auth_header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert 'invalid' in resp.json()['message'].lower() or 'format' in resp.json()['message'].lower()
